actionableComments.author: alice
actionableComments.body: Please mention this in the changelog.

instructions: Decide for each item under `## Review threads`, `## Actionable comments` whether a code change is warranted. **If any code changes are needed:** cancel in-progress runs first, apply edits, commit, rebase, push, then run the `resolve:` command. **If no code changes are needed:** skip cancellation/commit/push and run the `resolve:` command.
Apply code fixes: read and edit each file referenced under `## Review threads` and `## Actionable comments` above.
If you applied code edits: commit them with a descriptive message, then rebase onto `origin/main` per your repository's conventions before pushing.
Run the `resolve-only:` command shown above — no substitutions needed.
Before running the `resolve:` command, remove any thread from `--reply-thread-ids` if the latest visible comment in that thread is your own prior Shepherd reply. Do not reply to your own comments.

Run the `resolve:` command shown above, substituting `$HEAD_SHA` with the pushed commit SHA (or `$(git rev-parse HEAD)` if you did not push) and `$DISMISS_MESSAGE` with a one-sentence reply/description of what you changed.

Review the bodies shown under `## Review summaries (first look)` — you are seeing these for the first time. Eligible non-human IDs, when present, are already included in `--minimize-comment-ids` in the resolve or resolve-only command above; if any warrants a Shepherd Journal note, append it before running resolve.

For any large decisions or rejections you made this iteration, add or update a `## Shepherd Journal` section in the PR description (`gh pr edit 42 --body …`) summarizing each decision. For threads and comments, use the markdown link shown in its heading above; for reviews, reference the review ID. If this section already exists, append your entries under it instead of creating a duplicate heading.
Stop this iteration — if you pushed new commits, CI needs time before the next tick; otherwise stop before the next tick. Recheck: rerun `pr-shepherd 42` to recheck once after a fresh 30s–4m delay.
